meta.setting: Road sutler post at a dusty junction outside French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in supply pressure and road commerce

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Road movement, mining communities, and supply pressure.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park

image_prompt.subject: a road sutler post with crates, flour sacks, horse tack, ammunition boxes, and travelers bargaining under a patched awning
image_prompt.scene: dusty noon at a road junction; pack mules, water barrel, clerk behind counter, a woman checking a coffee tin, no readable labels
image_prompt.composition: square exterior shop plate with goods forming a cluttered frame around tense bargaining; road and pack animals remain visible beyond
image_prompt.camera: counter-corner view, close enough to read materials but not text, like an in-engine barter scene
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make every supply look useful and a little overpriced.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine supply-post scene
render_traits: slightly simplified travelers, mules, clerk, and goods silhouettes
modest texture resolution on canvas, sacks, wood, leather, brass, and dust
hard ambient occlusion under crates, awning, counter, and animals
hot noon light with awning shade
believable 1899 road supply objects and clothing
commerce-pressure composition rather than clean shop display
style_modifiers.palette: dust ochre, canvas tan, flour white, leather brown, brass dull gold, awning shadow, mule gray, and hot sky blue.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable labels, crate markings, prices, signs, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The sutler post, goods, travelers, pack mules, counter, and water barrel are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 road commerce.
The image shows supply pressure rather than clean merchandise display.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
